issues.body: fetch_bd_map() builds id_map from every issue containing a bd-id body marker, without requiring migration provenance. In apply_migration(), every id already in that map is then granted MIGRATION_LABEL. An unprivileged user can therefore create an issue containing a target marker before migration; a resumed --apply run will treat that issue as the migrated issue and attach the supposedly authentication-only label. The real issue is never created, and ci-close-merged-beads.py can later close the decoy. The new self-test covers an unlabeled decoy only at close time, not this label-conferring migration path.
issues.fix_hint: Do not backfill MIGRATION_LABEL onto arbitrary body-marker matches. Resolve resumptions from migration-owned state/provenance (for example the trusted checkpoint plus already-labeled issues), or otherwise validate that the existing issue was created by the migration before labeling it. Add a test in which an attacker-authored body marker precedes migration and assert that it is neither labeled nor accepted as the canonical mapping. Ambiguous or unverifiable pre-label mappings should fail closed for operator review.
issues.title: Resume path authenticates untrusted body-only mappings

summary: The issue-closing path is fail-closed once migration labels are trustworthy, but the migration resume path can itself authenticate an attacker-forged marker. This breaks the stated security boundary and can cause the canonical bead issue not to be created or closed.
